Slide 07 · Leadership Behaviors

A shared language
for development at every level.

The Leadership Behaviors Model is Houston Methodist's shared language for how we develop — built so that no matter what campus you're on, what department you're in, or what role you hold, we're all talking about the same things when we talk about great leadership.

The key message for you

Leadership here doesn't start with a title. It starts with how you show up today. The outer ring — Leading Self — applies to every single employee, no title required.

Three domains · Nine behaviors

Mission, Values & Culture

Transcript

Narration Transcript

Now let's talk about something that belongs to every single person in this room — not just people with "leader" in their title.

The Leadership Behaviors Model is Houston Methodist's shared language for how we develop. It was built so that no matter what campus you're on, what department you're in, or what role you hold, we're all talking about the same things when we talk about great leadership.

The model has three domains — Mission, Values and Culture… People… and Execution. Inside those, nine behaviors. And four tiers of leadership, from Leading Self all the way to Leading the Enterprise.

Here's the part that matters most for this module: the outer tier — Leading Self — applies to you. Right now. No title required. No promotion needed.

Leading Self is about how you show up today. Keep the wheel in mind as we walk through each domain in the slides ahead.
